_note: CANON: campfire stories, rumors, legends. Each tagged truth: true | exaggerated | nonsense — the PLAYER never sees the tag, and should never immediately know which is which (bible: Campfire Stories). `region` ties it to a place; `links` may point at a mystery:<id> or quest:<id>. Surfaced via the Codex + NPC examine/dialogue; pull rumors FROM here rather than inlining them. Enforced by world_coherence.mjs (valid truth tag, resolvable region).

lore.text: There's an idol under the old training yard, older than the Warren. On quiet nights it whispers — and the Elder swears it whispers *names*.
Everyone knows a goblin who eats a whole raw cabbage before a scrap can't feel fear. Everyone also knows what happens to that goblin's stomach.
Eat one of the big caps and you'll dream a stranger's whole day — their lunch, their arguments, the face of someone they loved. Nobody agrees whose memories they are.
At the Great Cap Festival the head judge has worn the same hat for forty years. It is a very good hat. This is not disputed.
Out in the deep bog, iron pumps the size of huts still turn, still draining water — into where, nobody's followed the pipes to find out.
To a bogling, rust is holy. Hand one a shiny new coin and a rusted nail and watch which they bow to.
Carry a lucky frog on the river and the bandits leave you be. The frog does not corroborate this.
The oldest oak in the Hollow chops *back*, they say. Three loggers claim to have seen it. All three were, by their own account, extremely tired.
There's a scarecrow in the far field that everyone agrees has moved. Nobody agrees where it started, or where it's going, or when it does it.
The ruin doors are cut too clean and sized too tall — for something bigger than a troll. The forest sprites tend the place and won't say who they tend it for.
The ridge trolls turn to stone by daylight, then spend the whole night arguing about whose fault it was.
The Red-Ears say Gorkholm's goblins have gone soft. Gorkholm says the Red-Ears learned to count last spring and haven't stopped bragging.
